summary: Gruber posts a housekeeping update covering two topics: a bug fix for the site's cookie-based preferences system and an update on Daring Fireball t-shirt sales. The preferences feature, which lets readers set custom font sizes, had a bug causing cookies to expire at session end rather than persisting for years. He also reports that shirt and membership sales have been strong since his April announcement of going full-time, and announces that two shirt designs are being discontinued.
key_points: A long-standing cookie expiration bug in the site's preferences system has been fixed, allowing font size choices to finally persist across browser sessions
T-shirt and membership sales have been crucial to sustaining Daring Fireball as a full-time operation since Gruber's April 2006 announcement
Two shirt designs ('Your PC Is a POS' and 'I✪DF') are being discontinued to simplify inventory, with a final order deadline given
Gruber highlights the community aspect of DF readers sharing photos of themselves wearing the shirts on Flickr
money_quotes: Shirt and membership sales have been helping to keep this operation afloat, whilst I write and continue working behind the scenes on upcoming initiatives to generate more moola.
Still haven't seen one that tops Eric Case's, though — he took his DF shirt to the 20,000-foot-high summit of Stok Kangri in northern India.
So if you want one of these but have been procrastinating on ordering, you'll need to place your order by the end of the day tomorrow (Friday August 18) to guarantee availability.
My sincere thanks for everyone's continuing support (and patience with regard to shipping delays).
My thanks to everyone who has supported my work — especially to those of you who had to (or continue to) wait a bit longer than "several weeks" for your shirts to reach you.
key_insight: This post captures the early bootstrapping era of Daring Fireball as an independent publishing business, sustained by reader merchandise purchases and memberships rather than traditional advertising.
